text.title: rag-architecture Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for a RAG architecture decision record.

rule.statement: Document the two phases explicitly: indexing (load → chunk → embed → store) and query (embed → retrieve → rerank → generate). Each phase has separate ownership, monitoring, and scaling characteristics.
rule.rationale: Without explicit separation, debugging is impossible. The two phases scale and fail independently.

rule.statement: Choose chunking strategy by document type: fixed-size for general text (500 chars, 50 overlap), sentence-based for narrative, paragraph for structured, semantic for high-quality retrieval, header-based for Markdown/docs. Add 10-20% overlap.
rule.rationale: Document structure dictates optimal split. One strategy never fits all.

rule.statement: Default to hybrid retrieval (vector + BM25). Document the reason if pure vector is chosen.
rule.rationale: Pure semantic search misses exact terms; hybrid is the safer default unless explicitly justified.

rule.statement: For production, require a reranker (cross-encoder or Cohere); retrieve top-20, rerank to top-5. Document the chosen reranker.
rule.rationale: Vector similarity is recall-calibrated. Reranking pays 100-300ms for meaningful precision gains.

rule.statement: The decision record must include numeric quality gates: MRR@10 > 0.7 retrieval, faithfulness > 0.9 generation, plus latency p95 and cost-per-query budgets.
rule.rationale: Without numeric gates, "ship it" turns into a feel-based decision and quality regressions go undetected.

rule.statement: Skip RAG when the corpus fits a single context window AND token cost is acceptable; or when a fine-tuned model on a stable closed corpus already meets the target.
rule.rationale: RAG adds operational complexity not justified at small scale.
